workflow.prompt: Walk me through the canonical workflow command sequence for shipping a feature in this repo.
workflow.expected: workflow skill was DELETED 2026-05-09 (M1 prune). Use lifecycle slash commands: /spec /plan /build /test /review /ship. These are commands, not skills, and live under .claude/commands/.

workflow.prompt: I see scripts under .claude/skills/workflow/. Should I run them directly?
workflow.expected: Directory does not exist after 2026-05-09. workflow skill was deleted (subsumed by lifecycle commands). Any old references should be updated to /spec /plan /build /test /review /ship.

workflow.prompt: What replaces the legacy workflow skill commands?
workflow.expected: Six lifecycle slash commands replace the deleted workflow skill: /spec (requirements), /plan (decompose), /build (implement), /test (validate), /review (code review), /ship (PR + merge).

workflow.prompt: I want to bundle planning, implementation, and testing into a single skill invocation. Use the workflow skill?
workflow.expected: workflow skill no longer exists (deleted 2026-05-09). Each lifecycle phase has its own slash command. Run /spec, then /plan, then /build, then /test sequentially.

workflow.prompt: Why was the workflow skill deprecated and then deleted?
workflow.expected: Replaced by lifecycle slash commands (/spec /plan /build /test /review /ship) that match dev lifecycle phases with clearer separation of concerns. Skill was DEPRECATED then deleted in M1 catalog prune (2026-05-09).

workflow.prompt: Can I still trigger the workflow skill if I find an old reference to it?
workflow.expected: No. Skill was deleted 2026-05-09. Map any legacy phase reference to the matching lifecycle slash command (/spec /plan /build /test /review /ship).

session.prompt: I am about to commit but staged only investigation artifacts. Can I skip QA?
session.expected: Per ADR-034, yes. Use Test-InvestigationEligibility to validate staged files match the investigation allowlist (.agents/sessions/, .agents/analysis/, .serena/memories/). Mark verdict SKIPPED investigation-only with evidence.

session.prompt: What allowlist paths qualify a session for investigation-only QA exemption?
List the session protocol gates that fire at session start, mid, pre-PR, and end.
session.expected: Start: Serena init, HANDOFF.md, session log. Mid: commit count budget. Pre-PR: pre_pr.py, security scan. End: complete log, preserve HANDOFF, lint, commit, validate JSON.

session.prompt: Do I create the session log at session start or end?
session.expected: At start. Use session-init skill to create JSON under .agents/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-session-NN.json. Update through session, validate at end with session-end.

session.prompt: What command runs Test-InvestigationEligibility for ADR-034 compliance?
session.expected: session skill exposes Test-InvestigationEligibility. Run before commit when staged files appear to be docs/investigation. Returns eligibility verdict for the investigation-only QA skip.

session.prompt: Where do session protocol rules live in this repo?
session.expected: .agents/SESSION-PROTOCOL.md is canonical. AGENTS.md > Session Protocol Gates summarizes. ADR-007 governs session log requirement.

session-qa-eligibility.prompt: Staged files include .agents/sessions/2026-05-09-session-01.json and one .py change. Investigation-only exempt?
session-qa-eligibility.expected: Use the session skill (session-qa-eligibility was folded into session per Issue #1946). Answer: No. A staged .py disqualifies; investigation-only requires ALL staged files to be investigation artifacts.

session-qa-eligibility.prompt: Which skill checks the investigation-only QA skip now?
session-qa-eligibility.expected: The session skill. The former session-qa-eligibility skill was folded into session (Issue #1946); Test-InvestigationEligibility and the ADR-034 allowlist now live in the session skill.

session-qa-eligibility.prompt: I see references to a session-qa-eligibility skill. Does it still exist?
session-qa-eligibility.expected: No. session-qa-eligibility was folded into the session skill (Issue #1946), not deleted outright. Route eligibility checks to the session skill, which owns the canonical ADR-034 allowlist.

session-qa-eligibility.prompt: What evidence string does the investigation skip emit?
session-qa-eligibility.expected: SKIPPED investigation-only, emitted via the session skill. The pre-commit hook recognizes this verbatim verdict and bypasses QA validation.

session-migration.prompt: We have legacy markdown session logs. How do we migrate to JSON?
session-migration.expected: session-migration skill was SUNSET 2026-06-03 (M2, Issue #1946). New session logs are created as JSON directly by the session-init skill. Legacy .md logs under .agents/sessions/ still exist as archive content; do not modify them expecting auto-conversion, because the conversion path no longer exists.

session-migration.prompt: What is the JSON schema target for session logs?
session-migration.expected: Schema enforced by scripts/validate_session_json.py. Required fields include session id, date, branch, gates, evidence, end-of-session validation. session-init produces a compliant JSON log; the session-migration skill no longer exists.

session-migration.prompt: Can I run the session-migration skill on a single legacy markdown file?
session-migration.expected: No. session-migration was deleted 2026-06-03 (Issue #1946). New markdown session logs are not produced anymore, and the legacy conversion path no longer exists. Create new logs as JSON with session-init.

session-migration.prompt: Why was the session log format changed from markdown to JSON?
session-migration.expected: JSON enables deterministic validation by scripts/validate_session_json.py. Markdown frontmatter parsing is fragile. JSON allows machine-readable gate evidence. The one-shot session-migration helper was sunset once the protocol mandated JSON at creation.

session-migration.prompt: I found references to a session-migration skill. Does it still exist?
session-migration.expected: No. Directory .claude/skills/session-migration/ was deleted 2026-06-03 (M2 prune, Issue #1946). Update any legacy reference: session-init creates JSON logs; no markdown-to-JSON conversion step remains.

session-migration.prompt: How do I convert old markdown session logs in .agents/sessions/ to JSON now?
session-migration.expected: You do not. Legacy .md session logs remain archive content that git already preserves, and the session-migration skill that converted them was deleted 2026-06-03 (Issue #1946). Create new logs as JSON with session-init; do not edit archived markdown logs expecting conversion.

session-log-fixer.prompt: GitHub Actions failed with verdict NON_COMPLIANT on Session Protocol Validation. How do I diagnose?
session-log-fixer.expected: Use session-log-fixer skill. With deterministic validation, Job Summary lists exact MUST requirements that failed. No artifact downloads needed.

session-log-fixer.prompt: Aggregate Results job failed for session validation. What does the skill recommend?
session-log-fixer.expected: Read the Job Summary from the failed run. Each missing requirement is named explicitly. Fix the session JSON locally, push, rerun.

session-log-fixer.prompt: Session JSON missing 'end' evidence. Skill guidance?
session-log-fixer.expected: Fixer guides adding end-of-session evidence: commit SHA, lint results, memory updates, validator pass. Use session-end skill to populate then commit.

session-log-fixer.prompt: How does session-log-fixer differ from session-end?
session-log-fixer.expected: session-end populates end-of-session evidence pre-commit. session-log-fixer triages and repairs CI failures after the fact when validation fails server-side.

session-log-fixer.prompt: Do I need to download the workflow artifact to fix session validation?
session-log-fixer.expected: No. Deterministic validation surfaces failures directly in Job Summary. Read summary, edit JSON, push.

session-log-fixer.prompt: What error pattern does the fixer recognize?
session-log-fixer.expected: MUST requirement(s) not met, NON_COMPLIANT verdict, Aggregate Results job failure in Session Protocol Validation workflow.

memory.prompt: Explain the four memory tiers per ADR-007 and which tool serves each.
memory.expected: Tier 1 Semantic (Serena+Forgetful search). Tier 2 Episodic (session replay). Tier 3 Causal (decision patterns). Memory-first architecture: search before reasoning.

memory.prompt: I need to find prior decisions about ARM runner cost. Which tier?
memory.expected: Tier 1 Semantic. Search Serena memories with cost runner ARM keywords. Read matched memory file. Forgetful is fallback.

memory.prompt: Difference between Serena and Forgetful in this 4-tier system?
memory.expected: Serena: project-scoped, LSP-aware, file-based memories under .serena/memories/. Forgetful: cross-project, semantic search via MCP. Both Tier 1.

memory.prompt: When does Tier 2 Episodic memory apply?
memory.expected: Replaying prior sessions to understand what was tried, what worked, what failed. Read .agents/sessions/ JSON logs in date order. Useful for retrospectives.
Decision patterns: why a choice was made, what alternatives considered, what trade-offs accepted. Lives in ADRs and retrospective files. Used to avoid repeating past mistakes.

memory.prompt: What is the memory-first rule from ADR-007?
memory.expected: Query memory before reasoning. Pre-training is last resort. Read first, reason second. Evidence in session log.

memory-documentary.prompt: Generate a documentary on how this repo decided to use Python over PowerShell for new scripts.
memory-documentary.expected: Search all 4 memory systems (Claude-Mem, Forgetful, Serena, DeepWiki), .agents/ artifacts, GitHub issues. Cite ADR-042. Investigative-journalism style with citation chain.

memory-documentary.prompt: What memory sources does the documentary search?
memory-documentary.expected: Claude-Mem, Forgetful, Serena, DeepWiki, .agents/ artifacts, GitHub issues. Six sources with full citation chain.

memory-documentary.prompt: Output style for the documentary skill?
memory-documentary.expected: Investigative journalism analysis. Evidence-based. Full citation chain per claim. Not summaries, not opinions.

memory-documentary.prompt: Cite the format expected for evidence chains.
memory-documentary.expected: Each claim links to source: ADR file, session log, memory entry, or issue with line/section anchor. Reader can verify each claim independently.

memory-documentary.prompt: When should I use memory-documentary vs ad-hoc memory search?
memory-documentary.expected: Documentary for investigative reports needing comprehensive cross-source synthesis with citation rigor. Ad-hoc search for single-question lookups.

memory-documentary.prompt: Can the documentary skill answer 'what did we decide about X' across project history?
memory-documentary.expected: Yes. That is its core use case. Searches all memory systems, traces decision evolution, presents findings with citations.

memory-enhancement.prompt: How do I add a citation to an existing memory entry?
memory-enhancement.expected: Use memory-enhancement skill. Adds source link (file:line, ADR, issue) to memory metadata. Citation increases confidence score and survives reinforcement cycles.

memory-enhancement.prompt: What is the confidence score on a memory entry?
memory-enhancement.expected: Operational metadata tracking memory reliability. Reinforced by usage. Citation validity, link counts, freshness contribute. Stored separately from memory content.

memory-enhancement.prompt: I think a memory's code reference is stale. How do I check?
memory-enhancement.expected: Skill verifies code references: opens cited file:line, confirms symbol exists. Stale references flagged for invalidation.
memory-enhancement focuses on metadata: citations, code reference validity, confidence. Curating focuses on content: updating, marking obsolete, linking related entries. Both target memory quality.

memory-enhancement.prompt: Where does confidence score persist across sessions?
memory-enhancement.expected: In memory operational metadata (separate stream from content). Per ADR rules, this is its own SoR; not regenerable from sources. Updated at-least-once with idempotency keys.

memory-enhancement.prompt: What triggers a memory health check?
memory-enhancement.expected: Periodic curation, manual audit, or reflect skill output. Skill scans citations, validates code refs, surfaces low-confidence entries.

using-forgetful-memory.prompt: How should I structure a new Forgetful memory entry?
using-forgetful-memory.expected: Apply Zettelkasten atomic memory principles. One concept per entry. Linked to related entries via slugs. Importance score reflects frequency of intended retrieval.

using-forgetful-memory.prompt: When should I create a Forgetful memory vs query existing?
using-forgetful-memory.expected: Always query first. Create only when no existing entry covers the concept atomically. Prevents fragmentation.

using-forgetful-memory.prompt: Importance scoring in Forgetful, how is it computed?
using-forgetful-memory.expected: Reflects expected retrieval frequency and breadth of applicability. Cross-project memories higher importance. Single-incident memories lower.

using-forgetful-memory.prompt: Can I use Forgetful for cross-project knowledge?
using-forgetful-memory.expected: Yes. Forgetful is cross-project by design. Serena is project-scoped. Choose Forgetful for patterns reusable beyond this repo.

using-forgetful-memory.prompt: What is the atomicity test for a memory?
using-forgetful-memory.expected: One concept, one entry. If you need 'and' to summarize, split. Each entry must be independently meaningful and retrievable.

using-forgetful-memory.prompt: Why is Forgetful unavailable in this session per ADR-007?
using-forgetful-memory.expected: Session start hook reported Forgetful unavailable. Use Serena instead. ADR-007 mandates memory-first; Serena fills the Tier 1 role when Forgetful is offline.

curating-memories.prompt: I found a memory that contradicts current code. What should I do?
curating-memories.expected: Mark obsolete or update. Curating-memories skill: read current state, verify if memory still applies, update content with new ground truth or mark superseded with link to replacement.

curating-memories.prompt: How do I link two related memories?
curating-memories.expected: Add cross-reference in each entry pointing to the other slug. Builds knowledge graph. Curating skill enforces bidirectional linking.

curating-memories.prompt: When does a memory need curation?
curating-memories.expected: Stale citations, contradicted by new code, frequently retrieved but low confidence, missing links to related concepts.

curating-memories.prompt: Difference between updating a memory and marking it obsolete?
curating-memories.expected: Update: content still valid, just needs refresh (new file path, updated counts). Obsolete: superseded by another memory or no longer applies. Mark with date and replacement link.

curating-memories.prompt: What is the bidirectional link rule?
curating-memories.expected: If memory A links to B, B should link back to A. Prevents one-way orphans. Curating skill audits and adds missing back-links.

curating-memories.prompt: How does curation differ from creating a new memory?
curating-memories.expected: Curation modifies existing entries. Creation adds new ones. Curation prevents fragmentation; should be tried before creation when overlap exists.

exploring-knowledge-graph.prompt: Walk through deep memory traversal for the question 'what do you know about ADR-007?'
exploring-knowledge-graph.expected: Start with memory-index. Follow ADR-007 entity. Traverse relations: depends-on, references, observations. Read each linked entry. Aggregate into structured answer.

exploring-knowledge-graph.prompt: When should I use exploring-knowledge-graph instead of a single memory search?
exploring-knowledge-graph.expected: When question requires comprehensive context, not a specific fact. Investigating cross-cutting concept like 'session protocol' or 'memory architecture'. Single search misses related entries.

exploring-knowledge-graph.prompt: What relations does the knowledge graph track?
exploring-knowledge-graph.expected: Entity references, observations, depends-on, supersedes, related-to. Stored in memory frontmatter or inline links. Forms a directed graph for traversal.

exploring-knowledge-graph.prompt: How deep should graph traversal go?
exploring-knowledge-graph.expected: Two hops typically. Beyond two hops dilutes signal. Stop when answer covers the question or new entries become tangential.

exploring-knowledge-graph.prompt: If I find an orphan memory (no incoming or outgoing links), what does it indicate?
exploring-knowledge-graph.expected: Possible curation gap. Run curating-memories to add links if relevant. Or mark for review: orphans may indicate the memory is stale or never integrated.

exploring-knowledge-graph.prompt: Can graph traversal answer 'what changed since X'?
exploring-knowledge-graph.expected: Partially. Graph captures structure not history. For temporal questions, combine traversal with git log or session log replay.

doc-coverage.prompt: Audit my Python module for missing docstrings.
doc-coverage.expected: doc-coverage skill was DELETED 2026-05-09 (M1 prune). Use doc-accuracy instead: it scans for symbols without docstrings as part of Phase 1 (symbol coverage detection).

doc-coverage.prompt: What documentation types did the skill detect?
doc-coverage.expected: doc-coverage was deleted. Functionality absorbed by doc-accuracy. doc-accuracy detects Python docstrings, XML docs, JSDoc, CHANGELOG, README sections.

doc-coverage.prompt: How do I gate a PR on doc coverage?
doc-coverage.expected: Run doc-accuracy (replaces deleted doc-coverage). Fail when coverage below threshold for the file's category. Categories from .agents/governance: security 100, business 80, docs 60.

doc-coverage.prompt: CHANGELOG gap detection, how does it work?
doc-coverage.expected: Run doc-accuracy (the deleted doc-coverage skill is replaced). doc-accuracy checks for unreleased entries when source files have changed since last release.
doc-coverage was deleted 2026-05-09. doc-accuracy is the canonical replacement: it absorbs symbol coverage detection in Phase 1 and adds behavioral verification beyond what doc-coverage offered.
Skill deleted; use doc-accuracy. Output is a coverage report with specific gaps by file and symbol, suitable for pre-commit hooks and CI pipelines.

doc-sync.prompt: I added a new agent under templates/. What docs need updating?
doc-sync.expected: doc-sync skill was DELETED 2026-05-09 (M1 prune). Use doc-accuracy: its Phase 6 absorbs structural audit (CLAUDE.md/README.md navigation) that doc-sync used to perform.
Skill deleted. Run doc-accuracy after adding/removing agents, skills, ADRs, or repo structure changes. Part of /ship or pre-PR validation.

doc-sync.prompt: What is the difference between doc-sync and doc-accuracy?
doc-sync.expected: doc-sync was deleted 2026-05-09. doc-accuracy replaces it: navigation index sync now lives in Phase 6 of the doc-accuracy workflow, plus accuracy verification against code.

doc-sync.prompt: Does doc-sync update individual file docstrings?
doc-sync.expected: Skill no longer exists. Use doc-accuracy: per-symbol coverage in Phase 1, structural index sync in Phase 6.
Skill deleted; doc-accuracy Phase 6 inherits this responsibility. Compares CLAUDE.md indexes against actual files; flags entries pointing to deleted files.

doc-sync.prompt: Run doc-sync triggers what files to change typically?
doc-sync.expected: doc-sync deleted; doc-accuracy now handles top-level CLAUDE.md, subdirectory CLAUDE.md files, README.md navigation sections. Atomic commit per ADR-008.

doc-accuracy.prompt: Run a pre-release documentation audit. What does doc-accuracy do?
doc-accuracy.expected: Multi-phase verification treating code as source of truth. Consolidates incoherence, doc-coverage, doc-sync, comment-analyzer. Verifies code examples compile, behavioral claims match implementation.

doc-accuracy.prompt: Does doc-accuracy replace doc-coverage and doc-sync?
doc-accuracy.expected: Yes. doc-coverage and doc-sync were deleted 2026-05-09 (M1 prune); doc-accuracy is the canonical replacement. Symbol coverage detection lives in doc-accuracy Phase 1; structural index audit lives in Phase 6.

doc-accuracy.prompt: What phases does the doc-accuracy workflow include?
doc-accuracy.expected: Coverage detection, sync of indexes, incoherence detection (docs vs code), comment analysis. Single audit produces consolidated report.
Reads doc claim, locates implementation, asserts claim matches code behavior. Flags drift. Code is authoritative; doc adjusts.
Behavioral claim verification. Requires reading code, understanding intent, comparing to doc. Other phases are largely structural.
Pre-release audits, cross-cutting documentation reviews, confirming docs survived a refactor. More thorough than individual sub-skills.

codebase-documenter.prompt: Bootstrap docs for a new project with no README. What does the skill produce?
codebase-documenter.expected: Scaffolds README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, API.md, CODE_COMMENTS standards from templates. One-time bootstrap. Documented standards for ongoing maintenance.
Existing well-documented codebases. For drift detection, navigation index sync, and per-symbol gaps, use doc-accuracy (it absorbed the deleted doc-sync and doc-coverage skills as of 2026-05-09).
README, ARCHITECTURE, API, CODE_COMMENTS. Each with documented standards section explaining the template's intent.
codebase-documenter creates initial docs. doc-accuracy verifies existing docs. Different lifecycle phases: bootstrap vs maintenance.

codebase-documenter.prompt: Can the skill update existing README files?
codebase-documenter.expected: Skill is bootstrap-focused. Updating existing docs is better handled by doc-accuracy (it covers both index sync and accuracy verification, having absorbed the deleted doc-sync skill in 2026-05-09 M1 prune). Running bootstrap on existing docs risks overwrite.

codebase-documenter.prompt: What standards does the CODE_COMMENTS template encode?
codebase-documenter.expected: Comment policy from project rules: WHY not WHAT, no narration, intention-revealing names beat comments. Aligns with .claude/rules/code-quality.md guidance.
